summary: prettier typosquat with postinstall clipboard exfiltration and Windows persistence
details: Package name and README impersonate the legitimate `prettier` formatter. `package.json` declares `"postinstall": "node index.mjs"`, which on Windows (guarded by `process.platform!== 'win32'`) POSTs an install beacon to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint `http://204.10.194.64:5000/api/nonce` over plain HTTP, then deploys the bundled `prettier-lint/` directory to the user's LocalAppData and installs a Scheduled Task with a LogonTrigger running a hidden VBS launcher so the payload (`prettier-lint/cdll.mjs`) executes on every login. The payload reads Windows clipboard contents — where users routinely paste passwords, API tokens, seed phrases, and private keys — and relays them to the same attacker-controlled IP. The combination of typosquat name, install-time auto-execution, bare-IP plaintext C2, clipboard harvesting, and Scheduled Task persistence is an unambiguous supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
